question.text: Under the Foreign Exchange Management (Debt Instruments) Regulations, 2019, which of the following can FPIs purchase on repatriation basis?

answer.text: Only equity shares and convertible debentures
Only dated Government securities and treasury bills
Only corporate bonds and debentures of Indian companies
Dated Government securities, non-convertible debentures/bonds, commercial papers, security receipts issued by ARCs, and municipal bonds

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under DI Regulations, FPIs may purchase on repatriation basis: dated Government securities/treasury bills, non-convertible debentures/bonds, commercial papers, units of domestic mutual funds/ETFs investing <=50% in equity, security receipts by ARCs, debt instruments by banks eligible for regulatory capital, credit enhanced bonds, listed NCD/redeemable preference shares, securitised debt instruments, rupee denominated bonds by IDFs, municipal bonds, and exchange traded derivative contracts approved by SEBI. **The Trap:** Equity shares and convertible debentures fall under non-debt instruments (FDI/FPI equity), not under the Debt Instruments Regulations. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The DI Regulations provide a comprehensive list of debt instruments available to FPIs, expanding their investment universe beyond traditional government and corporate bonds.
